---
title: "Test our prediction API"
format: html
echo: false
---

## Methodology & Architecture

This API serves a quantitative trading model trained for the Kalshi prediction market. It is the final endpoint of a fully automated MLOps pipeline.

The architecture relies on the following sequential process:

1. **Data Ingestion:** Extraction of historical candlestick data and volume imbalance for binary event contracts.

2. **Feature Engineering:** Computation of time-series features such as rolling price momentum, volatility, and liquidity metrics.

3. **Modelization:** A LightGBM regressor optimized via Bayesian search (Optuna) and validated through strict walk-forward cross-validation to prevent future data leakage.

4. **Signal Generation:** The model predicts short-term directional returns. The raw outputs are L1-normalized. This translates predictions into a strict capital allocation vector, dictating the exact portfolio weight assigned to each contract daily.


We can query our deployed model through the API below.

## Example prediction

```{ojs}
apiRoot = "https://kalshi-predictor.lab.sspcloud.fr"
predictionUrl = `${apiRoot}/predict?RET_1=-0.016363636363636358&VOL_YES_1=0.0&VOL_NO_1=0.0&RET_2=-0.023624197733169372&VOL_YES_2=0.0&VOL_NO_2=0.0&RET_3=-0.04324536190227335&VOL_YES_3=0.0&VOL_NO_3=0.0&RET_4=-0.00377456722634395&VOL_YES_4=0.0&VOL_NO_4=0.0&RET_5=0.004051228437010002&VOL_YES_5=0.0&VOL_NO_5=0.0&RET_6=-0.0306561945781606&VOL_YES_6=0.0&VOL_NO_6=0.0&RET_7=-0.00878955298844808&VOL_YES_7=0.0&VOL_NO_7=0.0&RET_8=0.011815525346207556&VOL_YES_8=0.0&VOL_NO_8=0.0&RET_9=-0.006186868686868574&VOL_YES_9=0.0&VOL_NO_9=0.0&RET_10=-0.0183440753594446&VOL_YES_10=0.0&VOL_NO_10=0.0&RET_MEAN_5=-0.016591306957682607&RET_STD_5=0.018373453107748947&RET_MEAN_10=-0.013511770105512733&RET_STD_10=0.0164399943474164&DIST_50=0.40659999999999996&DIST_MIN_20=0.0&DIST_MAX_20=0.9999999999339498`
```

```{ojs}
d3.json(predictionUrl)
```


## Daily prediction table

```{ojs}
pnlUrl = `${apiRoot}/predict/pnl`
pnlData = await d3.json(pnlUrl)

rows = Object.keys(pnlData.prediction.y_true).map(contract => ({
  Contract: contract,
  y_true: pnlData.prediction.y_true[contract],
  y_hat: pnlData.prediction.y_hat[contract],
  signal: pnlData.prediction.signal[contract],
  pnl: pnlData.prediction.pnl[contract]
}))
```

```{ojs}
Inputs.table(
  rows.map(r => ({
    Contract: r.Contract,
    y_true: r.y_true.toFixed(4),
    y_hat: r.y_hat.toFixed(4),
    signal: r.signal.toFixed(4),
    pnl: r.pnl.toFixed(4)
  }))
)
```

## Try it yourself

```bash
curl https://kalshi-predictor.lab.sspcloud.fr/predict/example
```

## API documentation

[https://kalshi-predictor.lab.sspcloud.fr/docs](https://kalshi-predictor.lab.sspcloud.fr/docs)
